Title: Functional characterization and structural basis of a reversible glycosyltransferase involves in plant chemical defence

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 UDP-glycosyltransferase (UGT), PF00201; PSPG motif-containing GT1 enzyme
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms target product and reversibly interconverts target product with its aglycone
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: tectorigenin + UDP-glucose; reverse reaction uses tectoridin + UDP
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant chemical defence; contributes to resistance against Rhizoctonia solani in rice leaf sheath assay in vivo and antifeeding activity against Spodoptera frugiperda; glycosides may also have health benefits for humans
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Highest in rhizome of B. chinensis; also detected in root, stem, leaf, flower, and fruit
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Bc7OUGT transcript highest in leaf and flower; very low in rhizome
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highest in leaf and flower; very low in rhizome
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyses regiospecific 7-O-glycosylation of tectorigenin using UDP-glucose to form tectoridin, and also catalyses the reverse deglycosylation reaction from tectoridin in the presence of UDP
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for reversible interconversion between tectorigenin and tectoridin; pathway bottleneck not formally established
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of tectoridin from tectorigenin and reversibly removes the sugar from tectoridin, thereby modulating defensive isoflavonoid composition
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: PacBio full-length transcriptome candidate mining; phylogenetic analysis; heterologous expression in E. coli; in vitro enzyme assays with HPLC-MS/MS product confirmation; kinetic analysis; reverse reaction assay; qRT-PCR; subcellular localization in Arabidopsis protoplasts; crystal structures; structure-guided mutagenesis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Enzyme activity tested across pH and temperature; maximal bidirectional activity at pH 6.5 and 42°C
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising enzyme for metabolic engineering of tectoridin and other glycosides; reversible activity enables aglycon exchange and production of valuable glycosides such as salidroside; could be used to engineer plant defence or microbial production systems
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Bc7OUGT is highly substrate-promiscuous toward multiple flavonoids/isoflavonoids, so engineering may cause off-target glycosylation shifts
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Catalytically reversible with Keq ≈ 0.93; broad acceptor promiscuity including genistein, quercetin, kaempferol, naringenin, chrysin, luteolin, apigenin, phloretin, and isoliquiritigenin; structural and mutagenesis data support catalytic roles of His18, Asp113, Thr135, and Glu384 and pocket-shaping roles of Phe144, Pro180, Pro182, and Met183
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Bc7OUGT (Bch3/12632.p1) was identified from B. chinensis as the main functional regiospecific enzyme catalysing 7-O-glycosylation of tectorigenin to tectoridin. 2. The purified enzyme also catalyses the reverse deglycosylation of tectoridin, demonstrating a rare reversible plant UGT activity. 3. Structural, kinetic, localization, and mutagenesis analyses defined the catalytic basis of substrate recognition and bidirectional activity. 4. The enzyme can be exploited for aglycon exchange and glycoside bioproduction.
